Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to create a process mining event log from a set of relational database tables Orders Order Lines Delivery Invoice Payments Products Resources The output must be a single table where each row is an event sorted chronologically per case order id

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Orders Table order id Case ID order date Start Event sales person id Resource customer type order priority

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order Lines Table order id item picking date Activity picker id Resource item id quantity Challenge Multiple rows per order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Do I create one event per item or aggregate per order The prompt suggests Items Picked events For process mining having one event per order for Picking Completed is often cleaner but Item Picked per line offers more granularity

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To keep the log manageable and focused on the Order lifecycle Case ID order id it is often better to aggregate picking into a single Order Picking Completed event per order OR list them as sub activities

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction Actually standard practice for O2C event logs usually aggregates the picking phase into one event per order to avoid clutter unless analyzing warehouse efficiency specifically But the prompt explicitly says Items Picked events I will generate one event per line item to be faithful to the data granularity provided

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Delivery Table order id delivery dispatch date Activity delivery arrival date Activity carrier id Resource One order can have multiple deliveries for example Order 1002 has DLV 5002 and DLV 5003 This means multiple Dispatch and Arrive events per case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice Table order id invoice issue date Activity billing person id Resource Order 1002 has two invoices

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payments Table invoice id links to Invoice which links to Order payment date Activity Need to join Invoice to Order to get Case ID

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Define the Schema Case ID order id Activity Name Derived from table context Timestamp The specific date time column associated with the activity Resource Mapped from ID to Name Role using the Resources Table Attributes Relevant extra info

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Order 1004 has no entry in the Delivery table I must include the events that exist Order Pick but note the absence of delivery invoice payment For the event log I only list events that happened

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Payment table does not have a resource ID I will mark Resource as Customer or External

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Refining Activity Names Orders Create Order Order Lines Pick Item Delivery Dispatch Dispatch Order Delivery Arrival Deliver Order Invoice Issue Invoice Payment Receive Payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Use the Resources Table to map IDs to Name and Role For Payments there is no resource ID I will label this Customer External For Carriers use Carrier Name Carrier

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Sorting Primary Sort Case ID to group by order Secondary Sort Timestamp chronological

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Handling Missing Data Assumptions Order 1004 stops at Picking This is an Incomplete Case I will include the events that exist Payment Resource Not in table Assumed Customer Item Picking I am creating one event per line item

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Attributes column can be a JSON string or separated columns A single Attributes column containing key value pairs is often cleaner for Markdown representation of varied data Or I can make specific columns

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Decision To keep the Markdown table clean and readable I will include Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource Item ID if applicable Transaction ID Delivery Invoice Payment ID Amount if applicable

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Re evaluating Columns The prompt asks for Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource Other Attributes I will create a column Attributes that contains a summary string

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually looking at standard Process Mining formats They usually have specific columns I will create specific columns for the most important linking IDs and a Details column for the rest

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait simpler is better for Markdown Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource Attributes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review of the Plan Map Resources using the Resources Table Map Payments to Orders via Invoices Sort by Time Format as Markdown Add a section for Assumptions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Payments Link PAY 2001 to INV 1001 to 1001 PAY 2002 to INV 1002 to 1002 PAY 2003 to INV 1003 to 1002 PAY 2004 to INV 1004 to 1003 PAY 2005 to INV 1005 to 1005

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check timestamps for Order 1002 Dispatch 1 01 04 Dispatch 2 01 05 Arrival 1 01 07 Arrival 2 01 08 Invoice 1 01 08 10 15 After Arrival 2 10 00 Yes Invoice 2 01 10 Payment 1 01 12 Payment 2 01 15 Looks consistent

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Formatting I will use a Markdown table I will ensure the Attributes column is readable I will format it as Key Value Key Value

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction To ensure the table is not too wide for Markdown rendering I will split Attributes into specific columns where possible or keep the string concise Proposed Columns Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource Reference ID Details I will also add Customer Type and Priority as columns

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final plan Columns Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource Reference ID Customer Type Order Priority Event Details This covers all requirements and allows for analysis
